name: Nectarine Ice
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash, peel and cut up enough nectarines to measure 4 cups.
2. Sprinkle the sugar and lime juice over the fruit; stir until well coated.
3. Set aside for at least one hour.
4. In a small bowl, sprinkle gelatin over cold water; stir and set aside to soften.
5. In a small saucepan, combine water with sugar; bring to a boil over high heat, stirring.
6. Lower the heat and keep at a slow boil for 5 minutes without stirring.
7. Remove from the heat and pour in softened gelatin; stir until gelatin has melted.
8. Set aside in the refrigerator to cool.
9. Spoon sugared fruit into a blender and blend until pureed.
10. Pour into a medium-sized bowl, remove sugar water from the refrigerator, and pour into the fruit.
11. Stir until well combined, cover, and freeze for 1 hour, or until mixture becomes icy.
12. Remove from freezer; uncover and stir to break up any icy pieces, then beat until smooth.
13. Pour into a freezer container or serving dish, cover, and freeze until firm.
14. Allow the ice to ripen in refrigerator for 2 to 2 1/2 hours before serving.
Makes 1 1/2 to 2 pints.
